memoryAudit.method: same-session baseline heap usage -> 3 scroll/nav/wait cycles -> forced Runtime.gc/HeapProfiler.collectGarbage after each cycle -> final heap snapshot summary

findings.title: LCP above good threshold (19.0s)
findings.suggestedFix: Improve LCP by prioritizing the hero/content resource, reducing render-blocking work, and optimizing server/critical CSS delivery.

findings.title: Total blocking time is high (564ms)
findings.suggestedFix: Reduce main-thread JavaScript by splitting, deferring, and removing non-critical third-party work.

principles.summary: Same-session memory pass retained +29.0 MB after interaction cycles and forced GC.

findings.title: Heap retained after interaction and GC (+29.0 MB)
findings.evidence: Same-session memory pass: baseline 23004656 bytes; final after interaction cycles + forced GC 65850408 bytes; delta 28985032 bytes; monotonic=false.
findings.suggestedFix: Profile repeated interactions, remove retained detached DOM/listeners/timers, and ensure route/component cleanup releases references.

verdict: Homepage batch: 4 principles with issues; top concerns: medium be-sustainable: Heavy transfer on initial load (17.4 MB); medium be-private-and-secure: Large third-party/network surface; medium respect-user-preferences: No explicit color-scheme/dark-mode signal observed.
_caveats: Scale batch: no Lighthouse, LCP or INP in this pass; coordinator CDP data should fill objective metrics.
Memory is a single heap snapshot, not a repeated-interaction leak proof.
Dark-mode/reduced-motion are inferred from probes unless separately captured.
